items.title: Drug charges laid after women allegedly try to evade police after exiting Spirit ferry

children.content: Two women are alleged to have had traffickable quantities of meth and cocaine in their car when they evaded police upon exiting the Spirit of Tasmania ferry in Devonport.

image.altText: A large quantity of illicit drugs and tobacco on a wooden table.

items.title: Half a billion litres of improperly treated sewage released into waterways

children.content: More than half a billion litres of improperly treated sewage were released into the state's waterways in the 2024 to 2025 financial year, including at popular swimming spots such as Launceston's Cataract Gorge.

image.altText: People lounge on the grass next to a natural body of water.

items.title: Neil the seal is back, bigger than ever — and that's a problem

children.content: Neil, an elephant seal well known to Tasmanians, has returned to land, with a new video showing him being coerced off a road for his own safety.

gallery.altText: Two people attempt to coax a large seal off a road.
A large seal chased off a road by a person with a board.

thumbnail.altText: Two people attempt to coax a large seal off a road.
A large seal chased off a road by a person with a board.

items.title: Serpentine leafminer detected in Tasmania for first time

children.content: The insect has been found at a commercial grower in Tasmania's north-west. It damages the leaves of the plants it attacks and can reduce crop yield.

image.altText: A fly-like insect with wings sits on a green leaf.

items.title: Two dead in car crash in Tasmania's south

children.content: Two vehicles collided at Old Beach in Tasmania's south earlier today. The two occupants of one of the vehicles have died at the scene, while the other driver is uninjured.

image.altText: Several police cars and an ambulance seen on a stretch of road that turns a corner, with a river on one side.

items.title: The public can soon see this album hidden away for 126 years

children.content: The Royal Society of Tasmania has owned an album of Owen Stanley's maritime artworks since 1900, but its delicate nature meant it could not be put on display. Now, the society is converting the album into a digital flip book so everyone can see it.

gallery.altText: A watercolour painting of a boat at a jetty, People watch on from the shore.

thumbnail.altText: A watercolour painting of a boat at a jetty, People watch on from the shore.

items.title: Proposal to replace 'barren wasteland' with apartments and car parks

children.content: A proposal for Hobart's waterfront known as 'Regatta Quay' has been put forward by Councillor John Kelly, which would create 3,200 car parks and 220 apartments.

image.altText: a render of people walking at a waterfront development

items.title: The potentially deadly substance being delivered to Aussie doors at all hours

children.content: The importation of nitrous oxide has exploded in the last decade. Nangs are increasingly being used by some to chase a high and are causing significant damage.

gallery.altText: A photo of a man sits atop a grave which is covered in flowers.
A collection of small metal bulbs.

thumbnail.altText: A photo of a man sits atop a grave which is covered in flowers.
A collection of small metal bulbs.

items.title: Hanson's Socceroos own goal should have been a free kick for Taylor

children.content: Pauline Hanson has chosen one of Australia's most popular national sporting brands and visible expressions of cultural diversity in her pitch for a "monoculture".

image.altText: Pauline Hanson stands in front of a blue backdrop. In front of her are two microphones.

items.title: Australians understand heatwaves. Europe may now be taking tips

children.content: Europe's record-breaking heatwave has highlighted its vulnerability to climate change. But countries could look to Australia's experience with scorching summers to learn valuable lessons.

image.altText: Four people playing in water near the Eiffel Tower

articles.title: Who is going to qualify for the World Cup knockout rounds?

children.content: The Socceroos will finish either second of third in their World Cup group. Second means qualification for the knockout rounds. Third is a bit more complicated.

image.altText: Aiden O'Neill (left) and Harry Souttar look on

articles.title: Key Socceroos cog ruled out with injury ahead of crucial Paraguay game

children.content: Wing-back Jacob Italiano is ruled out of Australia's crucial World Cup clash with Paraguay after suffering a groin injury.

image.altText: Jacob Italiano #4 of Australia controls the ball during the FIFA World Cup 2026

children.content: Haiti scores its first World Cup goal in 52 years, as Scotland's hopes of making it to the knockouts teeter against Brazil. Follow live.

items.title: Haiti stuns Morocco early as Scotland's World Cup hopes hang by a thread

updates.title: HT: Haiti holding Morocco 2-2 in first-half thriller

items.title: ASIO reveals new details on AUKUS espionage, warns of 'unprecedented threats'

children.content: Australia's domestic spy chief, Mike Burgess, has defended the agency's resourcing decisions in the years leading up to the Bondi terror attack, arguing his agency is grappling with an "unprecedented" volume of threats.

image.altText: Mike Burgess, Head of ASIO, generic

items.title: Karl Stefanovic expected to leave Channel Nine after Tommy Robinson podcast

children.content: Today show host Karl Stefanovic is set to leave Channel Nine after a podcast interview with British far-right figure Tommy Robinson, Nine Newspapers is reporting.

image.altText: A man in black sits behind a microphone.

items.title: France and Vanuatu clash over remote Pacific island where 'god resides'

children.content: Tensions are rising between Vanuatu and France as they negotiate a sovereignty dispute over two volcanic islands, believed to be inhabited by a god.

items.title: How a discarded coffee cup helped solve a 24-year cold case killing

children.content: Twenty-four years after the killing of a Canberra grandmother, a coffee cup discarded on an east coast road trip leads to the arrest and convictions of her killers.

image.altText: An older man and woman sitting on a bench and smiling.

items.title: Deadly bird flu discovered in second Australian state

children.content: A third confirmed case of the deadly H5 bird flu has been found in a migratory bird on Australia's southern coast.

image.altText: Warnings a much deadlier strain of avian influenza could arrive this spring

items.title: Sydney, Melbourne to lead price falls in 'fragmented' market

children.content: House price falls of up to 7 and 8 per cent in Sydney and Melbourne have been forecast for the year ahead, according to Domain. But it is far from doom and gloom for existing owners, with resale profits hitting a record.

image.altText: A sign in front of a building that reads 'Sold'

items.title: Small designers hitting roadblocks trying to get support against Shein copies

children.content: Small Australian fashion designers who have had their designs copied by overseas ultra-fast fashion companies say they have hit continuous roadblocks trying to get support.

image.altText: A composite shows a woman in a blue striped dress next to the Shein website selling the same dress.

items.title: Traffic cones on statues and a sea of orange: The traditions taking over the World Cup

children.content: Every four years, the World Cup provides the perfect backdrop for fans to showcase their culture's traditional football rituals in front of millions.

items.title: Police to review case of Epstein accuser Virginia Giuffre

children.content: The interactions Jeffery Epstein accuser Virginia Giuffre had with Australian police before her death will be reviewed, WA's police commissioner confirms.

items.title: France reports first Ebola case in doctor returning from DRC

children.content: France has confirmed its first case of Ebola, a doctor who had flown back from the Democratic Republic of Congo.

items.title: What happened when I embraced the Swedish right to camp anywhere you want

children.content: I've hated camping since I was a teenager, but embracing the spirit of Sweden's Allemansrätten — the right to camp wherever you like — changed everything.

children.content: Experts say the gambling industry is increasingly targeting women, and there are potential risks with a novelty bet.

items.title: Has One Nation won the 'family feud' of Australian conservatives?

children.content: There's a "family feud" in Australia's political right, and right now it appears One Nation is winning.

items.title: Bird flu has arrived in Australia

children.content: After years of waiting, the deadly H5N1 strain of bird flu has finally arrived in Australia.
Two birds found on the southern coast of Western Australia near Esperance have been confirmed as infected, but there is no evidence so far of a mass-mortality event.
So, what actually is H5N1, and why does it matter?

items.title: Will Congress demanding US military halt action in Iran end the war?

children.content: The US Senate approved a war powers resolution seeking to block military action against Iran on Tuesday. But don't expect the war to end.

children.content: A billionaire tech investor accused of assaulting his former partner and stealing expensive gifts he had bought her has his bail varied so he can visit his mother in Sydney.

items.title: Minister acknowledges housing market is experiencing 'correction'

children.content: Clare O'Neil has acknowledged that the property market is undergoing a correction, but says the government's proposed tax changes are not the sole cause.

items.title: Heartbroken Daryl Braithwaite will miss everything about performing

children.content: The ARIA Hall of Fame inductee speaks emotionally to the ABC about his decision to give up live performances, saying it has broken his heart to see how appreciative people have been.

items.title: Matt Wright sued for negligence after airboat allegedly crashed into tree

children.content: A man who says he suffered a "psychiatric injury" after witnessing injuries sustained by his wife in a 2023 airboat crash is suing tour operator and Netflix star Matt Wright for negligence.
